entities.comment: Node in the Chart of Accounts tree. Categorized by type (Asset, Liability, Equity, Revenue, Expense).

columns.comment: Alphanumeric account code (e.g. 1010, 5200); unique per org
Account name (e.g. Cash on Hand, Cost of Goods Sold)
Fundamental accounting type (ASSET, LIABILITY, EQUITY, REVENUE, EXPENSE)
FK to accounts — creates hierarchy (e.g. Cash under Current Assets)
Inactive accounts cannot be used in new journal entries

entities.comment: Physical store, warehouse, or logical division within an organization. Supports tree hierarchy via parentBranchId.

columns.comment: Display name of the branch
Physical address of the branch
Branch phone number
Branch contact email
Branch lifecycle status (ACTIVE, SUSPENDED, ARCHIVED)
If true, this is the organizations primary/headquarters branch
FK to branches — enables hierarchical branch structures

entities.comment: Physical or logical cash drawer/register/petty cash fund at a branch. Linked to a COA account.

columns.comment: Display name (e.g. Register 1, Petty Cash)
Current cash balance; updated in real-time with each movement
Corresponding general ledger account in the COA

entities.comment: Records each deposit, withdrawal, transfer, or adjustment on a cash account. Triggers journal entries.

columns.comment: The cash account affected
Nature of the cash movement (DEPOSIT, WITHDRAWAL, TRANSFER, ADJUSTMENT)
Movement amount (always positive; direction determined by type)
External reference (receipt number, bank slip, etc.)
Free-text notes

entities.comment: Customer who purchases goods. Supports deduplication via merge workflow.

columns.comment: Customers full name
Email address; optional but unique within org when provided
Phone number for contact or lookup at POS; unique within organization when set
Mailing or billing address
Customer lifecycle status (ACTIVE, INACTIVE, MERGED)
FK to customers — points to surviving customer after a merge

entities.comment: Tracks the current sequence value for a numbering rule per reset period. Atomically incremented when generating document numbers.

columns.comment: The numbering rule this counter belongs to
Organization scope
Branch scope (mirrors the rules branch scope)
Last used sequence number; next document gets currentValue + 1
Period identifier (e.g. 2026, 202604, or GLOBAL for NEVER)

entities.comment: Defines how document numbers are formatted for a specific document type. Pattern: {prefix}{date?}{sequence}{suffix?}.

columns.comment: The type of document this rule applies to (SALE, INVOICE, etc.)
Fixed string prepended to the number (e.g. INV-, SAL-)
Optional fixed string appended after the sequence
Whether to embed a date segment in the number
Date format string used when includeDate is true
Number of digits for the sequence portion, zero-padded
When to reset the counter back to 1 (NEVER, DAILY, MONTHLY, YEARLY)
Whether this rule is currently in use

entities.comment: Operational expense record. Workflow: DRAFT → APPROVED → POSTED. Posting auto-creates a journal entry.

columns.comment: What the expense is for (e.g. Office rent for April 2026)
Expense amount
The expense account to debit in the COA
FK to payables — if this expense created a vendor obligation
Workflow status (DRAFT, APPROVED, POSTED)
User who approved the expense
When approved
When posted and journal entry created
User who posted

entities.comment: Phiếu xuất hàng — goods issue from stock. Workflow: DRAFT → APPROVED → POSTED | CANCELLED

columns.comment: Auto-generated on posting
Source storage location
Reason for issuance (e.g. bán hàng, nội bộ, mẫu)
Free-text notes

entities.comment: Single item line within a goods issue document.

columns.comment: Parent goods issue document
Item being issued from stock
Quantity to issue (always positive)
Per-line notes

entities.comment: Tracks a bulk CSV import operation for items, opening balances, or adjustments. Enforces idempotency.

columns.comment: What type of data is being imported (ITEMS, OPENING_BALANCES, ADJUSTMENTS)
Original uploaded CSV file name
SHA-256 hash of the file content for integrity verification
Client-provided key to prevent duplicate submissions
Current processing state
Total number of data rows in the CSV
Count of rows that passed validation
Count of rows that failed validation

entities.comment: Individual row from a CSV import job. Stores raw data, validation status, and errors.

columns.comment: Parent import job
1-based row index from the CSV file
Parsed CSV row as a key-value object
Row processing status (VALID, ERROR, COMMITTED)
Array of { column?, code, message } objects describing validation failures

entities.comment: Junction: links an item (variant) to one option per attribute dimension.

columns.comment: FK to items — the variant item
FK to attribute definition (which dimension)
FK to attribute option (which value in that dimension)

entities.comment: User-defined product category label (per organization), used when assigning items.

columns.comment: Display name of the category (unique per organization)

entities.comment: A stockable product or material tracked in inventory. Identified by unique code per organization.

columns.comment: Short alphanumeric identifier (SKU) for the item
Human-readable product name
Detailed description or specifications
Unit of measure (e.g. pcs, kg, box)
Grouping label for filtering and reporting (e.g. Electronics, Furniture)
When false, item cannot be used in new transactions
Default purchase (cost) price per unit
Default selling price per unit
FK to inventory_providers — the supplier for this item
FK to products — null for legacy items without a parent product
Denormalized display label for variant attributes (e.g. "39 · Nâu")

entities.comment: Double-entry journal entry with balanced debit/credit lines. Auto or manually created. Supports reversal.

columns.comment: Auto-generated journal number (e.g. JNL-20260425-00001)
System module that created this entry (SALE, RETURN, MANUAL, etc.)
FK to originating document (sale, expense, etc.)
Human-readable description of the transaction
Internal notes not shown in reports
Whether this entry is active or has been reversed
Financial posting date (may differ from createdAt)
User who posted the entry
FK to journal_entries — the reversing entry (if reversed)
FK to journal_entries — the original entry (if this is a reversal)

entities.comment: Single debit or credit line within a journal entry. Sum of debits must equal sum of credits.

columns.comment: Parent journal entry
The ledger account affected
Amount debited to this account (0 if this is a credit line)
Amount credited to this account (0 if this is a debit line)
Per-line description (e.g. Revenue from sale SAL-001)
Display order of lines within the entry (1-based)

entities.comment: Specific slot within a storage (shelf, rack, bin, zone). Finest granularity for stock tracking.

columns.comment: Short identifier for the location (e.g. A-01-03)
Human-readable name (e.g. Aisle A, Rack 1, Shelf 3)
FK to storages — the storage this location belongs to
Physical type of the location (SHELF, RACK, BIN, ZONE)
Inactive locations cannot receive new stock

entities.comment: Top-level tenant entity. All business data is scoped to one organization.

columns.comment: Legal or trading name of the business; globally unique
Primary contact email for the organization
Optional phone number
FK to branches — the default/headquarters branch
Current lifecycle status; SUSPENDED blocks all access

entities.comment: Amount owed to a vendor/supplier. Lifecycle: DRAFT → POSTED → PARTIALLY_SETTLED → SETTLED.

columns.comment: Auto-generated or manually assigned reference number
Name of the vendor/supplier (free text; no vendor master yet)
Total amount owed to the vendor
ISO 4217 currency code
Payment deadline
Current lifecycle status (DRAFT, POSTED, PARTIALLY_SETTLED, SETTLED, VOIDED)
Expense or liability account to debit
Running total of all payments made against this payable
When the payable was posted to the books
User who posted

entities.comment: Records a single payment event against a payable. Multiple settlements for partial payments.

columns.comment: The payable being paid
Amount of this payment
Date the payment was made
Payment method (e.g. CASH, BANK_TRANSFER, CHECK)
External reference (bank txn ID, check number, etc.)

entities.comment: Payment tendered against a POS sale. Supports split tender (multiple payments per sale).

columns.comment: The sale this payment applies to
Payment method used (CASH, CARD, CREDIT, OTHER)
Amount tendered
External reference (card auth code, credit account ID, etc.)

entities.comment: Atomic permission seeded at startup (e.g. inventory.item.create). Global, not org-scoped.

columns.comment: Machine-readable permission key following module.resource.action convention
Human-readable explanation of what this permission allows
Logical module grouping for UI display (e.g. inventory, accounting, pos)

entities.comment: Cashier's working shift on a POS terminal. Must be opened before recording sales. Lifecycle: OPEN → ACTIVE_SALES → CLOSING → CLOSED.

columns.comment: Optional reference to a physical terminal/register device
Current session lifecycle state (OPEN, ACTIVE_SALES, CLOSING, CLOSED)
Cashier who opened the session
When the session was opened
User who closed the session
When the session was closed
Cash amount in the register at session start (float/seed money)

entities.comment: A dimension of variation for a product (e.g. Size, Color).

columns.comment: FK to products — the product this attribute belongs to
Attribute dimension name (e.g. Size, Color)
Display ordering among sibling definitions

entities.comment: A specific value within an attribute dimension (e.g. "39", "Nâu").

columns.comment: FK to the parent attribute definition
Display label for the option value
Display ordering among sibling options
Short code appended to SKU when generating variant codes

entities.comment: A product grouping that may have multiple item variants (SKUs). Org-level, not per-branch.

columns.comment: Human-readable product name
Detailed product description
Inactive products are hidden from catalog
Default supplier for variants of this product
True if created by legacy migration script (TKT-036)

entities.comment:
Maps a product to exactly one location within a storage.
Constraint: one product can only be stored in one location per storage (warehouse).

columns.comment: FK to products
FK to storages — which warehouse
FK to locations — assigned position in that storage

entities.comment: Supplier / vendor who provides items to the organization.

columns.comment: Short alphanumeric code unique per organization
Human-readable provider name
Contact email address
Contact phone number
Free-text notes or address info
Inactive providers cannot be assigned to new items

entities.comment: Phiếu đặt hàng — purchase order from supplier. Workflow: DRAFT → APPROVED → RECEIVING → RECEIVED | CANCELLED

columns.comment: Auto-generated on approval
Supplier (inventory_providers)
Destination storage location for received goods
Expected delivery date
Free-text notes

entities.comment: Single item line within a purchase order.

columns.comment: Parent purchase order
Item being ordered
Quantity ordered from supplier
Quantity already received into stock
Unit purchase price
Per-line notes

entities.comment: Amount owed by a customer to the organization. Supports partial settlement and write-offs.

columns.comment: Reference number
The customer who owes the amount
Total amount owed by the customer
ISO 4217 currency code
Payment deadline
Current lifecycle status
Revenue or asset account to credit
Running total of all payments received from the customer
When posted to the books
User who posted
Explanation when the receivable is written off as uncollectible

entities.comment: Records a single payment received from a customer against a receivable.

columns.comment: The receivable being paid
Payment amount received
Date payment was received
Payment method (e.g. CASH, BANK_TRANSFER)
External reference

entities.comment: Self-service registration workflow for new organizations or branches.

columns.comment: Whether this is an organization or branch registration
Freeform JSON containing submitted registration details (org name, owner email, etc.)
Current state in the approval workflow
Admin who approved or rejected the request
When the review decision was made
Explanation provided when the request is rejected

entities.comment: Return transaction against a previously completed sale. Triggers stock restoration and updates sale status.

columns.comment: Auto-generated return receipt number
The original sale being returned against
The current POS session processing the return
Sum of return line totals before tax
Total tax refunded
Total refund amount
Stated reason for the return
When the return was processed

entities.comment: Single item line in a return. References the original sale line. Stock added back to location.

columns.comment: Parent return document
The original sale line being returned
The item being returned
Location where returned stock is placed back
Quantity being returned (always positive)
Unit price at time of original sale
Total refund for this line (quantity x unitPrice)

entities.comment: Named authorization role scoped to an organization (e.g. Admin, Cashier).

columns.comment: Tenant that owns this role
Human-readable role name, unique per org
Optional longer explanation of the roles purpose
If true, role was auto-created during org setup and cannot be deleted

entities.comment: Join table linking a role to a permission. Determines which API actions a role can perform.

columns.comment: The role receiving the permission
The permission being granted

entities.comment: Completed POS sale transaction with line items and payments. Can be fully or partially returned.

columns.comment: Auto-generated receipt number (e.g. SAL-20260425-00001)
The POS session this sale belongs to
Optional customer linkage for loyalty/receivables
Sum of all line totals before tax
Total tax amount across all lines
Final amount after tax (subtotal + taxAmount)
Current sale status (COMPLETED, RETURNED, PARTIALLY_RETURNED)
Timestamp of the sale

entities.comment: Single item line in a POS sale. Stock deducted from the specified location on completion.

columns.comment: Parent sale transaction
The product sold
Inventory location from which stock was deducted
Quantity sold
Price per unit at time of sale
Total for this line (quantity x unitPrice)
Tax amount for this line

entities.comment: Links a user (sales manager) to a branch where they have managerial authority over sales.

columns.comment: The user assigned as sales manager
The branch under this managers authority
Organization scope
When the assignment was created
Who made the assignment

entities.comment: Links a user (salesperson) to a branch where they are authorized to conduct sales.

columns.comment: The user assigned as a salesperson
The branch where the user operates
Organization scope for tenant isolation
Timestamp of assignment creation
Admin or manager who created this assignment

entities.comment: End-of-session cash count reconciliation. Compares expected vs actual cash. Variance requires manager approval.

columns.comment: The session being reconciled
System-calculated expected cash in the register
Actual counted cash reported by the cashier
Difference: actualCash minus expectedCash (positive = over, negative = short)
Whether a manager has approved the variance
Manager who approved the variance
When the variance was approved
Explanation for the variance

entities.comment: Display/sales floor area linked to a branch and backed by a storage.

columns.comment: Showroom display name
FK to storages — the storage backing this showrooms inventory
If true, this is the branchs primary showroom

entities.comment: Document for correcting stock quantities at a location. Workflow: DRAFT → PENDING_APPROVAL → POSTED.

columns.comment: Auto-generated document number
The location being adjusted
Machine-readable reason (e.g. DAMAGE, RECOUNT, THEFT)
Human-readable explanation
Current workflow status (DRAFT, PENDING_APPROVAL, POSTED, CANCELLED)
User who approved the adjustment
When approved
User who posted the adjustment
When posted and ledger entries created
Additional notes

entities.comment: Single item line in a stock adjustment. Quantity is signed: positive = increase, negative = decrease.

columns.comment: Parent adjustment document
The item being adjusted
Signed quantity change: positive = increase, negative = decrease
Per-line notes

entities.comment: Denormalized current stock quantity per item per location. Updated on every ledger posting.

columns.comment: The item being tracked
The location holding the stock
Current on-hand quantity; can be negative in rare adjustment scenarios
Timestamp of the most recent stock movement affecting this balance

entities.comment: Immutable audit log of every stock movement. Append-only; corrections are offsetting entries.

columns.comment: The item affected by this movement
The location affected by this movement
Categorizes the reason for the stock change
Signed quantity: positive = stock in, negative = stock out
Source document type (e.g. SALE, TRANSFER, ADJUSTMENT)
UUID of the source document
Optional human-readable note
When this movement was financially posted

entities.comment: Document authorizing movement of inventory between locations/branches. Workflow: DRAFT → APPROVED → POSTED.

columns.comment: Auto-generated document number via DocumentNumberRule
Location from which stock is being sent
Location receiving the stock
Branch of the source location
Branch of the destination location
Current workflow status (DRAFT, APPROVED, POSTED, CANCELLED)
User who approved the transfer
When the transfer was approved
User who posted (finalized) the transfer
When the transfer was posted and ledger entries created
Free-text notes about the transfer

entities.comment: Single item line within a stock transfer document.

columns.comment: Parent transfer document
The item being transferred
Quantity to transfer (always positive)
Per-line notes

entities.comment: Physical warehouse or storage area within a branch. Contains multiple Locations.

columns.comment: Storage name (e.g. Main Warehouse, Back Storage)
If true, this is the branchs default storage for receiving goods

entities.comment: Assigns a user as a manager of a specific storage within a branch.

columns.comment: The user being assigned as storage manager
The branch containing the storage
The storage being managed
Organization scope
When the assignment was created
Who made the assignment

entities.comment: Associates a user with a branch they are authorized to operate in.

columns.comment: The user being assigned to a branch
The target branch
Organization scope for tenant isolation
When the assignment was created
Admin user who made the assignment

entities.comment: Human operator who can authenticate and perform actions in the system.

columns.comment: Tenant the user belongs to; used for row-level security filtering
Login email address; unique within the organization
Bcrypt hash of the users password; never exposed via API
Users given name, shown in UI and reports
Users family name
When false the user cannot log in or perform any action; acts as a soft-delete
Timestamp of the users most recent successful authentication

entities.comment: Join table linking a user to a role within an organization.

columns.comment: The user receiving the role
The role being assigned
Organization context for this assignment
When the role was assigned
